# Getting Started with LexPad

A quick guide to get productive in five minutes.

## 1. Launch LexPad

```bash
./scripts/run-lexpad.sh              # Development build
open dist/LexPad.app                 # After building the app bundle
LexPad myfile.txt                    # Open from Terminal
```

## 2. Open and save files

| Action | Shortcut |
|--------|----------|
| Open file | `Cmd+O` |
| Quick Open (fuzzy search) | `Cmd+P` |
| Save | `Cmd+S` |
| Save As | `Cmd+Shift+S` |

Drag files onto the window to open them. Unsaved tabs show a dot in the title.

## 3. Work with tabs

- **New tab:** `Cmd+T` or double-click empty space in the tab bar
- **Rename tab:** double-click the tab title
- **All open tabs:** click **▼** on the right of the tab bar
- **Close tab:** click **×** on the tab

## 4. Find and navigate

| Action | Shortcut |
|--------|----------|
| Find | `Cmd+F` |
| Replace | `Cmd+Option+F` |
| Go to line | `Cmd+L` |
| Command palette | `Cmd+Shift+P` |

Use the command palette to discover any feature by name.

## 5. Customize

Open **Settings** (`Cmd+,`) to set:

- Font and tab size
- Word wrap and line numbers
- Theme (30+ built-in color schemes in **View → Appearance → Style Configurator**)
- Keyboard shortcuts (macOS or Notepad++ preset)
- Session restore on launch

## 6. Split view

**View → Split Horizontal** or **Split Vertical** to edit two files side by side. Drag the divider between panes to resize. Drag a tab to an editor edge to split.

## 7. Side panels

Toggle from the **View** menu:

- **Workspace** — folder tree for a project
- **Function List** — jump to symbols in the current file
- **Git Panel** — status, diff, and commit

Drag panel edges to resize.
